mentions.note: Defense contractor to Denmark (F-35, military equipment); NATO commitments support demand; tariff risks but geopolitical tensions favor defense.
Bob asks about defense contractors with reasonable dividend and long-term appeal. I like Lockheed Martin; always confused by Trump's no-buyback/no-dividend threat but still works here.
#7 January performer, up 31%. Defense surge on Trump 50% budget increase (Doge disbanded). Excellent Q with top/bottom beat, terrific forward guidance. CEO Jim Telt awesome.
Down 15% in 3 weeks despite Trump's big orders and backlog; Cramer avoids because if war is truly over (as Trump suggests), defense spending will weaken.

CEO Jim Taiclet does great job. Stock down past 3 weeks = terrific opportunity. War escalation and bigger defense budget incoming = strong tailwind. Straightforward defense buy; manufacturer of weapons being used in current Iran conflict.
Jeff bought at $674, down to $608; Cramer sees war environment as tailwind—buy more, stock will be 'terrific' on defense spending.
Flagship missile systems (THAAD, Aegis BMD) used across Middle East, Ukraine, Israel defense; also sells previous-gen fighter jets to allies; key for missile stockpile replenishment.
Thursday earnings could be blockbuster; CEO Jim Taiclet handling government orders exceptionally; plenty more to come; buy here even if war ends; defense spending momentum unstoppable.
Best defense stock in group; while Cramer not recommending defense broadly, LMT is preferred choice if exposure desired given scale and sustainable cash generation.
High school caller concerned about US conflict risks; Cramer recommends scaled entry: buy 2 shares now around 523, then 3 more if stock pulls back below 500.
